steps.description: Press the F1 key to activate the Command Palette in the Visual Studio Code interface
Type text: 'Terminal:Create New Terminal' in Visual Studio Code's Command Palette to execute the command for initializing a new terminal instance.
Press the Enter key to confirm the "Terminal: Create New Terminal" command selection from the dropdown menu in the Visual Studio Code Command Palette.
Type text into the integrated terminal in Visual Studio Code: 'open https://teams.microsoft.com/v2'.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the command `open https://teams.microsoft.com/v2` in the integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code within the "Terminal" tab.
Click on the "Use another account" option in the Microsoft "Pick an account" login interface to initiate signing in with a different account.
Click on the "Email, phone, or Skype" input field within the Microsoft "Sign in" page.
Type text: ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_ACCOUNT}}  into the email address input field on the Microsoft account sign-in page.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the email input and proceed by interacting with the "Next" button on the Microsoft login page.
Type ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_ADMIN_PASSWORD}} into the password input field on the Microsoft login page (URL: login.microsoftonline.com).
Press the "Enter" key to submit the password and trigger the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login interface.
Click the "Yes" button in the "Stay signed in?" prompt on the Microsoft login page.
Click the "Apps" icon in the left sidebar of the Microsoft Teams interface to access additional applications.
Click the "Manage your apps" link in the left-side navigation panel of the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Get more apps" button under the "Manage your apps" section within the "Apps" view of Microsoft Teams. The button is visually represented as a "+" icon next to the text.
Click on the "Upload an app" option in the dropdown menu located under the "Manage your apps" section of the Microsoft Teams Apps interface, near the coordinates (609, 354).
Click the "Upload a custom app" button within the "Upload an app" dialog in the Microsoft Teams app interface.
Click the "Open" button located at the bottom-right corner of the file selection dialog, used to confirm the file selection (file type filter: *.ZIP, *.zip).
Click the "Open" button located in the bottom-right corner of the file selection dialog to confirm the selection of a folder or file.
Click the "Open" button located in the bottom-right corner of the file selection dialog.
Click the "Open" button at the bottom-right corner of the file selection dialog to confirm the selection of the highlighted "build" folder.
Click the "Open" button in the file selection dialog to confirm the selection of the highlighted file, "appPackage.dev.zip".
@assertion 'Add or Open' button is exist
